document: A Comprehensive Guide to the Law of Confidence and Breach of Confidence in Singapore_ Insights from [2024] SGCA 16 - IRB Law Singapore (Singapore - Row 33)

sections.heading: Whether these claims could be made in respect of the same set of confidential
sections.summary: This section addresses whether claims for breach of confidence can be made regarding the same set of confidential information. It explains that the modern law of confidence establishes an independent cause of action for misuse of confidential information, distinct from traditional approaches like the Coco test which requires proving specific elements such as the plaintiff's relationship to the information and the defendant's misuse.

requirements.text: The protection of confidential information has evolved significantly over time.
Historically, it found expression in various forms such as breach of contract and the tort of inducing breach of contract.
The modern law of confidence, however, firmly establishes an independent cause of action for the misuse of confidential information.

sections.source_quote: 2. Whether these claims could be made in respect of the same set of confidential information. The protection of confidential information has evolved significantly over time.
